reviewer: hand-authored (Opus) — driven headless-Chrome end-to-end run + desktop+mobile+light/dark screenshots

rubric.evidence: Driven headless run (fresh profile, served copy): clicked Download (~770 MB) → model loaded on WASM → clicked Extract on the 'Person & role' example → real filled JSON {"Name":"Maria Garcia","Role":"Chief Technology Officer","Company":"Northwind Robotics","Location":"Berlin"} (23 tokens, backend WASM), badge '✓ valid JSON (JSON.parse OK)'. Console + network clean (0 errors). Source loads the text-generation pipeline in a Web Worker and generates greedily from the template-conditioned prompt — no canned result.
rubric.notes: Verified runnable BEFORE building: dtype sweep found the q8/int8/uint8 exports degenerate (immediate EOS → empty output) but q4 (onnx/model_q4.onnx) produces valid extraction JSON on WASM. The demo uses q4 accordingly and states this honestly.

rubric.evidence: Output is JSON matching the user's template, filled with spans copied verbatim from the source text; validated on the page with JSON.parse() (green 'valid JSON' badge / red 'not valid JSON' on failure). 'See inside' shows the exact template-conditioned prompt (<|input|> … ### Template … ### Text … <|output|>) and the raw decoded output before parsing.
rubric.notes: Expected shape (a JSON object mirroring the template) is correct and legibly surfaced; the practical page exercises array fields (line items, skills) and the wild page nested objects.

rubric.evidence: conformance-derived: declares-model-id (onnx-community/NuExtract-1.5-tiny-ONNX linked to card), declares-task (text-generation), declares-backend (WebAssembly/CPU), declares-quantisation (q4), inference-off-main-thread (worker.js). At-a-glance names the Qwen2 architecture, the fine-tune base, dtype q4 (onnx/model_q4.onnx) with the degenerate-quant caveat, ~770 MB, MIT license.
rubric.notes: Backend/dtype match what worker.js loads (device:'wasm', dtype:'q4'). Honest disclosure incl. that it is NOT a chatbot but a template-conditioned extractor.

rubric.evidence: First visit: loader 'download-required' with a single 'Download model (~770 MB)' button — no silent transfer. Warm reload in the same profile: loader auto-inits to 'ready' with the ONLY action button 'Clear cached model' (verified warm_loader.buttons == ['Clear cached model'], no Download). Uses the shared createModelLoader.
rubric.notes: Matches the auto-init contract exactly: absent → labelled Download; cached-current → auto-init, no button; per-model clear-cache present.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region (role=status aria-live), errors-on-the-page (.err status + Retry via loader). Extract shows 'Extracting on the CPU…' then 'Done.'; an invalid template is caught (worker validates JSON.parse of the template and posts a readable error); generation failures set status .err. The output panel turns red with the raw text when the model's output isn't valid JSON — honest, not hidden.
rubric.notes: Progress/ready/error wired accessibly. Offline-cached reuse and a forced partial/evicted cache path were not separately driven this pass (openQuestions).

rubric.evidence: Main page: example chips (aria-pressed), editable source-text + JSON-template textareas, Extract, valid/invalid badge, see-inside prompt + raw-output panels. Basics/Practical/Wild: type chips + two textareas + Extract. Multi-model: OCR loader + NuExtract loader + sample thumb + 'Read image with TrOCR' + editable recognized-text + template + Extract. Primary buttons min-block-size:44px; chips 40px.
rubric.notes: controls-operable is manual-evidenced; a full keyboard-only traversal (tab order + visible focus across all 5 pages) is the remaining verification.

rubric.evidence: Before a run the output panel shows a muted placeholder and the loader shows the honest absent-model state on a fresh profile. The visible JSON was produced by the on-device generation (23 tokens observed) and validated with JSON.parse on the page. The wild page deliberately shows invalid output in red rather than faking success.
rubric.notes: No pre-baked result anywhere; validity is proven live, not asserted.

rubric.evidence: conformance: images-have-alt (multi-model sample thumbnail has alt; no other content ), buttons-have-names (all buttons have text). Status uses role=status aria-live=polite; output + raw panels aria-live=polite; every textarea has an associated
rubric.notes: Strong baseline. A screen-reader pass over the streaming raw-output panel and the JSON result would further validate.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check: desktop clean · mobile clean. Driven matrix on the main page: scrollWidth<=innerWidth at 1280×800 AND 360×740 (DPR3, touch). The text/template pair uses grid auto-fit minmax(min(100%,20rem),1fr) so it stacks on mobile; textareas box-sizing:border-box + min-inline-size:0; json-out and prompt panels use overflow-wrap:anywhere and internal overflow-y. Light + dark screenshots read and confirmed.
rubric.notes: Support seeded needs-review by the automated pre-scan; this driven pass is the evidence to flip main-page desktop+mobile to ok. Sub-pages share the same grid + CSS.

rubric.evidence: All generation runs in a Web Worker (worker.js) with token streaming to the raw-output panel, so the CPU-bound 0.5B decode never blocks the page. inp-stays-low is manual-evidenced (off-thread architecture).
rubric.notes: An explicit INP measurement while it generates would confirm; the off-thread architecture is the key mitigation. CPU extraction of a short field set completed quickly (23 tokens) with a clean console.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use grid auto-fit with minmax(min(100%, …), 1fr) and set min-inline-size:0 / box-sizing:border-box on fields so paired text areas stack on narrow viewports without overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the text/template pair is a grid with repeat(auto-fit, minmax(min(100%,20rem),1fr)); textareas set inline-size:100%, min-inline-size:0, box-sizing:border-box; json-out/prompt panels use overflow-wrap:anywhere + internal scroll. Verified no horizontal overflow at 360×740 and 1280×800.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check desktop+mobile clean; driven overflow check true/true; screenshots read

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates aria-live
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state changes in an accessible live region; label every control; keep buttons named and keyboard-operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live=polite loader/status; output + raw panels aria-live=polite; labelled textareas; example chips grouped with aria-label + aria-pressed toggling.

guidanceConsulted.query: stream text output without INP regressions long tasks
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy/CPU-bound generation off the main thread and stream incrementally so it doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — generation runs in models/nuextract-extraction/worker.js and streams tokens to the raw-output panel via TextStreamer; the main thread only appends text and parses the final JSON.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; driven run stayed responsive; console clean

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Associate every control with a <label>; use appropriate sizing and spellcheck=false for code/JSON input.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — each source-text and JSON-template textarea has an associated <label>; template textareas use spellcheck=false and a monospace font; the multi-model recognized-text box is editable and labelled.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: DOM inspection; buttons-have-names + labelled fields; driven extract produced valid JSON

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied (inherited) — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (shared shell); all pages link the shared stylesheet and crumb back to the catalogue/model page.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: shared design system; crumbs on every page

openQuestions: Keyboard-only traversal across all 5 pages: focus order, visible focus ring on chips/textareas/buttons, and SR announcement of the streamed raw output and final JSON.
Drive the partial/evicted cache and offline-cached states explicitly (delete one cached file → expect Re-download; offline warm cache → still runs).
How robustly does the 0.5B q4 model hold nested templates on the hardest wild inputs? Capture the invalid-JSON rate honestly across the wild examples.
Multi-model: confirm the TrOCR → NuExtract chain end-to-end on a real device (both models cached) and measure combined latency.

followUpGoals.goal: Keyboard + screen-reader matrix pass for nuextract-extraction across all 5 pages.
Add a WebGPU fast-path variant page for NuExtract (q4f16) to compare tok/s vs the WASM CPU path on the same extraction.
Add an ASR → NuExtract multi-model composition (spoken note → structured JSON) alongside the OCR one, as a new stable slug.

summary: nuextract-extraction is a genuinely-distinct, honest demo: NuExtract-1.5-tiny is a Qwen2.5-0.5B fine-tune specialised for template-conditioned structured extraction — NOT a general chat LLM like the built Qwen pages. Verified runnable BEFORE building via a dtype sweep (q8/int8/uint8 degenerate → empty output; q4 produces valid extraction JSON) and driven end-to-end in headless Chrome: fresh profile shows a labelled ~770 MB Download with no silent transfer, a real filled JSON object validated with JSON.parse (green badge), a see-inside panel exposing the exact <|input|>…<|output|> prompt and raw decode, warm reload auto-inits to ready with only a Clear-cache button, clean console, and no horizontal overflow on desktop or mobile. Five pages: basics (named fields), practical (invoice/résumé/email with array fields), wild (nested templates + messy multi-entity, honest about invalid JSON), multi-model (TrOCR image→text → NuExtract text→JSON). Revision 1.
